summary: Matched the game-mode pause overlay module by replacing four placeholder gmpause functions with typed implementations, adding the PauseData struct, and updating prototypes to use concrete HSD_GObj/JObj-related types. The implementation loads the GmPause archive scene data, builds and animates the pause UI JObj tree, toggles visibility for LRAS/Z/analog-stick prompts based on flags, and rotates the analog stick/background based on controller input for the active slot.
what_changed: Implemented fn_801A0E34, gm_801A0FEC, gm_801A10FC, and fn_801A1134 in src/melee/gm/gmpause.c instead of leaving them as placeholder match comments.

Added local/static data used by the matched functions, including lbl_80479B10 as struct PauseData, jobj assertion/string literals, a deg2rad constant, archive/gobj externs, and the GmPause archive name.
fn_801A0E34 now reads HSD_PadMasterStatus for the stored player slot, scales normal stick X/Y by 10.0f, converts to radians with 0.017453292F, and applies JObj rotations.

gm_801A0FEC now records the slot, requests the appropriate animation frame, and uses JOBJ_HIDDEN flag toggles to show or hide LRAS, Z, analog stick, analog stick outline, and background prompts according to a flag bitmask.
gm_801A10FC now resets the pause data slot sentinel to 99 and hides the background JObj.

fn_801A1134 now loads the GmPause archive and ScGamPause_scene_data, creates the GObj, loads the root JObj, finds child JOBJs by index, attaches the JObj to the GObj, sets up GX link/proc callback, runs initial animation, and initializes the slot sentinel.
Updated gmpause.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS placeholders to concrete signatures: fn_801A0E34(HSD_GObj*), gm_801A0FEC(s32, u8), gm_801A10FC(void), and fn_801A1134(void).
Added baselib forward declarations to gmpause.h and gm/types.h so HSD_GObj and HSD_JObj pointers can be used without pulling full baselib definitions into the headers.
Added struct PauseData to gm/types.h with JObj pointers for background, analog_stick, lras, z, analog_stick_outline, plus a signed slot field.
smart_moves: Introduced a small PauseData struct instead of keeping the pause overlay state as untyped globals, making the matched code easier to read while preserving offsets.
Used baselib/forward.h in headers to type HSD_GObj and HSD_JObj pointers without unnecessary heavy includes.
Kept the slot sentinel value 99 explicit in both the update and reset paths, documenting the inactive/no-slot behavior visible in the code.
Modeled the prompt visibility as direct JOBJ_HIDDEN flag manipulation, matching how pause UI pieces are conditionally shown based on match-rule flags.
Preserved important asset/archive strings such as GmPause and ScGamPause_scene_data, which are key evidence for matching the scene-loading routine.
The child lookup sequence in fn_801A1134 captures the UI hierarchy indices for analog stick, LRAS, Z, and outline, giving future work concrete anchors for the pause scene structure.
decomp_lessons: When matching UI overlay code, reconstructing the state holder struct can clarify repeated JObj accesses and naturally expose field offsets.

Asset-loading functions often require awkward pointer nesting, as shown by HSD_Joint**** joint and HSD_JObjLoadJoint(***joint); preserve the pointer shape that matches codegen even if it looks unusual.
Header cleanup from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete prototypes is valuable once call signatures are known, especially for GObj proc callbacks and game-mode entry points.
For JObj animation code, visibility changes frequently compile as HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll/HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll around JOBJ_HIDDEN rather than higher-level UI abstractions.

Controller-driven UI animation can require exact float constants and sign conventions; this PR used 10.0f scaling and 0.017453292F degree-to-radian conversion, with Y rotation applied to the analog stick and negative X rotation applied to the background.
Forward declarations are often enough in shared type headers when fields are only pointers, reducing include churn during matching.
naming_conventions: Matched but still-unknown functions retained address-style names such as fn_801A0E34 and gm_801A0FEC.
The new state struct was named PauseData, with descriptive field names matching pause UI pieces: background, analog_stick, lras, z, and analog_stick_outline.
Static labels and externs retained generated lbl_ address names where semantic names were not yet established, such as lbl_80479B10, lbl_804D6700, and lbl_804D6704.
Header prototypes use concrete C signatures and address comments in the existing project style, for example /* 1A0FEC */ void gm_801A0FEC(s32, u8);
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8), suggesting stack padding was needed to preserve the original stack layout for fn_801A0E34.
Kept the literal deg2rad constant 0.017453292F and also declared lbl_804DA984, indicating float literal placement/order mattered for matching.
Preserved static assertion/string literals related to JObj quaternion checks, likely to reproduce rodata ordering required by HSD_JObjSetRotation calls.
Used explicit casts such as (u8)lbl_80479B10.slot and (u8) HSD_GObj_804D7849 to steer code generation.
Used exact unsigned constants in GObj creation/setup calls, including GObj_Create(0xEU, 2U, 0U), GObj_SetupGXLink(..., 0xBU, 0U), and HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54(..., 0U).
Kept the archive load call shape lbArchive_80016DBC("GmPause", &joint, "ScGamPause_scene_data", 0), including the nested HSD_Joint pointer type.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The context reports one issue comment but no human text excerpt was included, so no specific reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename fn_801A0E34 or fn_801A1134 to semantic gmpause function names.
Search for other uses of struct PauseData or lbl_80479B10 to see whether the pause overlay state was refined.
Check callers of gm_801A0FEC to document the meaning of the u8 flag bits for LRAS, Z, and analog stick prompts.
Search for subsequent cleanup of the static JObj assertion strings and lbl_804DA984 deg2rad constant.
Compare other archive scene-loading matches that use lbArchive_80016DBC and nested HSD_Joint pointer types.
